title: Minimize access to create persistent volumes

input_row.title: Minimize access to create persistent volumes
input_row.description: The ability to create persistent volumes in a cluster can provide an opportunity for privilege escalation, via the creation of hostPath volumes. As persistent volumes are not covered by Pod Security Admission, a user with access to create persistent volumes may be able to get access to sensitive files from the underlying host even where restrictive Pod Security Admission policies are in place.
input_row.rationale: The ability to create persistent volumes in a cluster opens up possibilities for privilege escalation and should be restricted, where possible.
input_row.audit: Review the users who have create access to PersistentVolume objects in the Kubernetes API.
input_row.remediation: Where possible, remove create access to PersistentVolume objects in the cluster. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/rbac-good-practices/#persistent- volume-creation

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_rbac_persistentvolume_create_permissions_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate all ClusterRole and Role objects in the cluster using the Kubernetes API (kubectl get clusterroles, kubectl get roles --all-namespaces).
- For each ClusterRole and Role, inspect the rules[].resources and rules[].verbs fields.
- Identify any ClusterRole or Role that grants "create" permission on the "persistentvolumes" resource (rules[].resources includes "persistentvolumes" AND rules[].verbs includes "create").
- Enumerate all RoleBinding and ClusterRoleBinding objects to determine which users, groups, or service accounts are bound to these roles.
- Output a list of all subjects (users, groups, service accounts) that have "create" access to PersistentVolume objects, either directly or via aggregation.
- Pass Criteria: Only authorized administrative users or tightly controlled service accounts have this permission. Fail if any broad, unnecessary, or wildcarded subjects (e.g., system:authenticated, system:serviceaccounts, or *) are granted this permission.
- Example kubectl commands:
  - kubectl get clusterroles -o yaml | grep -B5 -A10 'resources:.*persistentvolumes' | grep 'verbs:.*create'
  - kubectl get roles --all-namespaces -o yaml | grep -B5 -A10 'resources:.*persistentvolumes' | grep 'verbs:.*create'
  - kubectl get clusterrolebindings -o yaml
  - kubectl get rolebindings --all-namespaces -o yaml

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.10 PDF
